reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — isolated transformers.js verify (real cosines), headless first-visit page drive (real 6×6 matrix + search), conformance runner, responsive matrix, desktop screenshot read

rubric.evidence: Isolated verify on the exact transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build (headless Chrome, WASM/no-GPU): pipeline('feature-extraction','onnx-community/embeddinggemma-300m-ONNX',{dtype:'q8'}) loaded and returned real 768-d vectors — with EmbeddingGemma's prompt prefixes, cos(query,'cat rested' doc)=0.7077, cos(query,'kitten napped' doc)=0.6886, cos(query,'quarterly revenue' doc)=0.3667 → related > unrelated. Full page drive (fresh profile): clicked Download (~300 MB) → 'ready' → Embed rendered a real 6×6 = 36-cell cosine matrix (diagonal 1.00, attention-pair 0.67 vs unrelated 0.44), backend WASM, 1200 ms; search returned 6 ranked rows. 0 console errors, 0 network failures. Same worker path (models/embeddinggemma/worker.js) as the page.
rubric.notes: First embedder on the Gemma 3 backbone genuinely runs under transformers.js 3.7.5 (WASM q8). The instruction prefixes (query vs document) are applied in the worker and materially separate related from unrelated.

rubric.evidence: Output shape matches feature-extraction: one 768-d vector per input (readout dim=768; verify dim=768). Similarity-matrix diagonal is exactly 1.00 and off-diagonals lie in [-1,1]. 'See inside' renders the real vector strip + pre-norm magnitude + token count / 2048. Matryoshka panel truncates to 512/256/128 and re-normalizes, showing cosine preserved.
rubric.notes: pooling:'mean', normalize:false in the worker, then L2-normalize in JS so cosine = dot product — the trained EmbeddingGemma pooling.

rubric.evidence: Desktop screenshot confirms at-a-glance: model google/embeddinggemma-300m linked, run via onnx-community/embeddinggemma-300m-ONNX, task feature-extraction, backbone Gemma 3 (~308M) → 768-d, 100+ languages, instruction prompts shown verbatim, mean pooling, Matryoshka 768→512→256→128, context 2048, quantization q8, backend WASM, size ~300 MB, license Gemma Terms (linked). Inference off-main-thread in worker.js.
rubric.notes: The four distinctions from the other built embedders — Gemma 3 backbone, multilingual, instruction prompts, Matryoshka — are all stated and accurate. License honestly flagged as the custom Gemma Terms, not Apache.

rubric.evidence: Fresh profile: loader showed single action 'Download model (~300 MB)' — no silent auto-download. After load the run/search controls enabled via onReady. Uses the shared createModelLoader on every page; the multi-model rung uses two independent loaders (embedder + bge-reranker) with the reranker loading on demand.
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract: absent → labelled Download; a current cached copy would auto-init with no button (shared loader behaviour, exercised on the other built embedder pages).

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live regions on every page; the loader announces checking→downloading→ready with a progress bar during the ~300 MB download; a .status.err surfaces embedding/search/rerank failures on the page.
rubric.notes: Offline-cached and partial-eviction paths are handled by the shared loader but not separately driven here.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled controls across the ladder: passages textarea, Embed/Reset/Search buttons, query input (Enter bound), example chips, the query-vs-document toggle (basics), the Matryoshka size range slider with a live <b> readout (practical), top-k number input (multi-model). All native, keyboard-operable; the Matryoshka slider re-ranks instantly from cached vectors.
rubric.notes: A full driven keyboard-traversal pass on the slider + toggle at both viewports is the remaining agent check; the main matrix + search path is verified end-to-end.

rubric.evidence: On a fresh profile the matrix/search/inspect areas are empty and the loader shows the honest download state — nothing canned is presented as live output. The 36-cell matrix, the 6 ranked search rows, and the Matryoshka cosines all come from real on-device runs.
rubric.notes: Confirmed no pre-baked result; cross-lingual (wild) and retrieve→rerank (multi-model) both call the real workers.

rubric.evidence: conformance a11y assertions pass: every button has a text name; no without alt. The similarity matrix is a real with scope=col/row headers + per-cell title; the PCA scatter SVG has role=img + aria-label; the Matryoshka slider has an associated label + aria-describedby hint. Desktop screenshot read: warm-paper/indigo design system, legible in dark theme.
rubric.notes: Matrix high-similarity cells use accent-ink for contrast in both themes.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check.mjs: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — scrollWidth ≤ innerWidth, no clipped controls, console clean, network clean. The wide matrix is wrapped in .matrix-scroll (overflow-x:auto) and grid-2 collapses to one column; the slider row and chips wrap.
rubric.notes: support set to needs-review pending a driven interaction pass at mobile; layout parity itself is clean and the wide matrix scrolls inside its own container.

rubric.evidence: All embedding + reranking inference runs in Web Workers off the main thread. Main-thread maths (cosine, power-iteration PCA-2D, spherical k-means, Matryoshka truncation) runs over a handful of already-computed 768-d vectors, cheap enough to stay responsive. Page drive showed a 1200 ms embed with the UI responsive.
rubric.notes: Architecture is INP-friendly; an explicit INP measurement during Embed would confirm.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use grid repeat(auto-fit,minmax()), flex-wrap and overflow-x:auto on wide content to avoid page-level horizontal overflow; prefer logical properties + intrinsic sizing.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — grid-2 uses minmax(300px,1fr) auto-fit; the wide similarity matrix scrolls inside .matrix-scroll rather than overflowing the body; the Matryoshka slider row and example chips wrap; widgets use inline-size/block-size logical properties.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy synchronous work off the main thread (workers) / yield so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — feature-extraction runs in models/embeddinggemma/worker.js; the multi-model rung adds the built bge-reranker worker; only light vector maths + Matryoshka truncation run on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; page drive stayed responsive during the ~300 MB download and 36-cell matrix render

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/inference failures in a live region the user can read, not only the console.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — embedding/search/rerank failures set a .status.err in a role=status aria-live region; the shared loader exposes a Retry path.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: status/rowStatus role=status aria-live in every page; loader error state

openQuestions: Drive a full keyboard traversal at mobile + desktop: is the Matryoshka slider reachable with a visible focus ring and does its <b> readout announce; is the query-vs-document toggle operable by keyboard?
How much retrieval quality does q8 cost vs fp16 on EmbeddingGemma, and how far can Matryoshka be pushed (128-d) before recall drops on a real corpus?
The cross-lingual (wild) demo asserts an English query ranks a same-meaning foreign passage above a same-language distractor — worth capturing that ranking screenshot as evidence.

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for embeddinggemma: drive the Matryoshka slider + query/document toggle + search on mobile+desktop, capture the cross-lingual ranking, then flip support to ok (or unsupported+evidence).
Add a 'Matryoshka recall curve' wild demo: over a fixed corpus, plot retrieval recall@k at 768/512/256/128 dims so the storage-vs-quality tradeoff is quantified, not just asserted.

summary: embeddinggemma is a strong, honest demo of a genuinely distinct embedder: real feature-extraction via transformers.js in a Web Worker on Google's Gemma 3 backbone (multilingual, instruction-prompted, Matryoshka 768→128), verified to load and produce a real 6×6 cosine matrix (related 0.67 > unrelated 0.44) and to rank passages by meaning, with a fresh-profile isolated verify showing related 0.71/0.69 > unrelated 0.37 using the query/document prompt prefixes. Accurate at-a-glance disclosure of the four distinctions from the other built embedders, honest absent-model Download state (no silent ~300 MB transfer, verified), clean mobile+desktop layout with the wide matrix scrolling in its own container, and a four-rung ladder (basics similarity + prefix toggle → practical on-device search with a live Matryoshka size dial → wild cross-lingual search → multi-model retrieve-then-rerank with the built bge cross-encoder). License honestly flagged as the custom Gemma Terms. Remaining work is a driven slider/toggle interaction pass before flipping support from needs-review to ok. Revision 1.
